entry.title: Multicollinearity or Omitted Variable Bias? Answers to a Seeming Conundrum

link.title: Multicollinearity or Omitted Variable Bias? Answers to a Seeming Conundrum

entry.summary: In statistics or causal inference courses, students are usually taught a number of “threats” they need to watch out for when running regression analyses, such as multicollinearity, omitted variable bias, reverse causality, measurement error, selection bias, etc. They often form the impression that the presence of these threats will “bias” their results (more specifically, regression estimations). In this blog post, I discuss a seeming conundrum involving multicollinearity and omitted variable bias.
